meta.title: Mental Maths Mastery for Kids, Number Sense, Not Tricks (Ages 5-10)
meta.description: Live online mental maths course for ages 5-10: number bonds, decomposition strategies and estimation built into real number sense, the honest alternative to abacus and trick-based programs. 8 one-hour classes a month, free demo first.
meta.level: Ages 5-10 (roughly grades K-5), all starting levels
meta.commitment: 2 live one-hour classes per week + 10 minutes of daily play-practice
meta.certification: Course completion certificate + stage mastery checks

program_overview.title: Mental Maths Mastery for Kids
program_overview.tagline: Number sense, not tricks. The speed follows, and it never expires.
program_overview.description: A 12-month live program that builds genuine mental arithmetic in children aged 5-10, the honest alternative to abacus franchises and 'Vedic tricks' courses. Trick-based speed impresses at demos and quietly expires: it bypasses understanding, so it cannot transfer to fractions, algebra or problem solving. We build number sense instead, numbers as decomposable quantities, number bonds, compensation and estimation, through games and puzzles a child genuinely enjoys. The result is a child who calculates fast because they see numbers clearly, and that clarity keeps paying through every year of school mathematics.
what_makes_this_different: Number sense over tricks: strategies a child can explain always beat procedures they can only perform
The speed is real and durable, decomposition, bonds and compensation transfer straight into fractions and algebra later
Games, puzzles and number talks: children wait for these classes, which is the engagement no drill sheet buys
Concrete-pictorial-abstract progression, the method behind the world's strongest maths systems
Explain-back in every class: what a child can teach, a child owns
One dedicated mentor who knows exactly which facts and strategies each child holds
A genuine on-ramp: graduates flow into our grade-level maths programs already ahead
One full interactive hour per class, twice a week, built for young attention spans with variety, not lectures

learning_path.phase_1: Number sense foundations: quantities, bonds to 10 and 20, and the part-whole thinking everything else stands on
learning_path.phase_2: The strategy toolkit: make-ten, doubles, compensation, and multiplication as structure, not chanting
learning_path.phase_3: Big-number courage: multi-digit mental strategies, fraction sense beginnings, estimation mastery and speed with understanding

career_outcomes: A child who volunteers answers in maths class instead of hiding
Arithmetic fluency that makes every later topic, fractions, percentages, algebra, feel lighter
Estimation judgment: the life skill of knowing when an answer looks wrong
A genuine head start into grade-level mastery programs and competition maths

phase_1_foundation.title: PHASE 1: Number Sense Foundations (Months 1-4)
phase_1_foundation.description: Before speed comes seeing: numbers as quantities that break apart and recombine. This phase builds subitizing, part-whole thinking and the bonds of 10 and 20 through concrete materials and games, tuned to each child's starting stage.

month_1.title: Month 1: Seeing Numbers
month_1.week_1: Playful diagnostic: which facts, strategies and confidence the child already holds, disguised as games
month_1.week_2: Subitizing and quantity sense: dots, dice and ten-frames, recognising amounts without counting
month_1.week_3: Part-whole thinking: every number is made of other numbers (7 is 5-and-2, 6-and-1, 3-and-4)
month_1.week_4: The number line as home: position, order, distance and the empty-number-line game

month_2_3.title: Months 2-3: The Bonds That Power Everything
month_2_3.week_5_6: Bonds of 10 to automaticity, through games, never flashcard grind
month_2_3.week_7_8: Doubles and near-doubles as anchor facts; adding 9 and 11 by compensation preview
month_2_3.week_9_10: Bridging through 10: the make-ten strategy that turns 8+5 into 10+3
month_2_3.week_11_12: Bonds to 20 and fact families: addition and subtraction as one idea seen from two sides

month_4.title: Month 4: Subtraction as Distance + Stage Checkpoint
month_4.week_13: Subtraction two ways: taking away versus finding the gap, and when each is faster
month_4.week_14: Counting up for change: the shopkeeper's method every adult secretly uses
month_4.week_15: Mixed strategy play: number talks where children compare routes to the same answer
month_4.week_16: Stage mastery check through games: solve it, explain it, teach it back

phase_2_strategies.title: PHASE 2: The Strategy Toolkit (Months 5-8)
phase_2_strategies.description: The heart of mental maths: named strategies the child chooses between, make-ten, compensation, doubling and halving, plus multiplication met as structure and arrays rather than chanted tables. Choice is the skill; speed is the side effect.

month_5.title: Month 5: Two-Digit Courage
month_5.week_17_18: Adding two-digit numbers mentally: jumps of ten, then the leftover ones
month_5.week_19_20: Compensation as a superpower: 39 + 26 becomes 40 + 25; subtracting 19 by taking 20 and giving one back

month_6.title: Month 6: Multiplication as Structure
month_6.week_21_22: Multiplication as groups and arrays, seen and built before recited
month_6.week_23_24: The clever tables route: 2s, 10s and 5s first, then doubling to 4s and 8s; 9s by compensation

month_7.title: Month 7: The Full Tables, Owned
month_7.week_25_26: 3s, 6s and the tricky squares; the handful of hard facts isolated and conquered
month_7.week_27_28: Division as the other side of multiplication; fact-family fluency through games

month_8.title: Month 8: Doubling, Halving and Strategy Choice
month_8.week_29_30: Doubling and halving chains: 16 x 5 as 8 x 10, the strategy that feels like magic and is pure structure
month_8.week_31_32: Strategy tournaments: same problem, different routes, and the metacognition of choosing, plus stage mastery check

phase_3_mastery.title: PHASE 3: Big Numbers, Estimation and Durable Speed (Months 9-12)
phase_3_mastery.description: The toolkit scales up: multi-digit mental arithmetic, the beginnings of fraction sense, estimation as judgment, and, now that understanding holds, genuine speed work. The course closes with each child measurably faster and, more importantly, able to explain every move.

month_9.title: Month 9: Multi-Digit Mental Arithmetic
month_9.week_33_34: Hundreds without fear: place-value jumps, friendly numbers and compensation at scale
month_9.week_35_36: Mental multiplication beyond tables: 23 x 4 by splitting, 15 x 6 by halving-doubling

month_10.title: Month 10: Estimation as Judgment
month_10.week_37_38: Rounding with purpose: getting close fast, and knowing how close is close enough
month_10.week_39_40: The reasonableness habit: catching wrong answers by sense, the skill calculators cannot replace

month_11.title: Month 11: Fraction Sense Beginnings
month_11.week_41_42: Halves, quarters and the fraction-of-a-quantity game, mental fractions from pictures
month_11.week_43_44: Fraction and decimal friends: one-half is 0.5 is 50%, the bridge built early

month_12.title: Month 12: Speed With Understanding + Graduation
month_12.week_45_48: Timed play now that meaning holds: beat-your-own-record ladders, never child-versus-child pressure
month_12.week_49_52: Graduation project: each child runs a number talk, teaching their favourite strategy, plus final stage mastery check and the road-ahead plan

faqs.question: How is this different from abacus or Vedic maths classes?
faqs.answer: Those programs teach procedures that produce fast answers, and the speed is real, but it lives outside understanding. It rarely transfers to fractions, word problems or algebra, and it fades when practice stops. We build number sense instead: strategies the child understands and chooses between. The speed arrives a little later and then never leaves, because it is made of understanding.

faqs.question: What age is right to start?
faqs.answer: Ages 5-10, and the mentor meets each child at their actual stage rather than their age. Five-year-olds start with quantity games and bonds; nine-year-olds might start at the strategy toolkit. The playful diagnostic in the free demo places every child accurately.

faqs.question: Will my child actually enjoy this, or is it drilling in disguise?
faqs.answer: Enjoyment is the engine of the whole method: number talks, strategy tournaments, beat-your-own-record ladders and games carry every class. Parents consistently tell us their children wait for these classes, which sounds impossible for arithmetic until you watch one. That is what the free demo is for.

faqs.question: My child counts on fingers past age 7. Is that a problem this fixes?
faqs.answer: Yes, and gently. Finger counting past grade 2 means number bonds never became automatic, so every calculation starts from scratch. We rebuild the bonds through games until known facts replace counting, without ever shaming the fingers on the way.

faqs.question: Does this replace school maths or run alongside it?
faqs.answer: Alongside, and underneath: number sense is the foundation school maths quietly assumes. Children in this course typically find school arithmetic suddenly easy, which buys the confidence that changes everything else.

faqs.question: How much practice is needed between classes?
faqs.answer: About ten minutes a day of assigned play, a game, a strategy challenge, a number talk with a parent. Small daily doses beat weekend marathons for fluency building, and ten minutes keeps it joyful.

faqs.question: What does the course cost?
faqs.answer: Small-group classes are ₹1,499 per month and private 1-on-1 mentorship is ₹4,999 per month in India; international students pay USD $100 per month for group and $150 for 1-on-1. Both include 8 live one-hour classes a month, recordings, and a free demo class before any payment.

faqs.question: Can we try it before paying?
faqs.answer: Yes. Every child starts with a free live demo class that doubles as the playful diagnostic, no card details, no obligation.

testimonials.name: Sonam Oswal, mother of Dhairya (verified Google review)
testimonials.text: My child Dhairya is really enjoying the classes. This is his first online class, and he eagerly looks forward to it. I can see his improvement.

testimonials.name: Shewta Singh, mother of Ishan (verified Google review)
testimonials.text: My son struggled with math for years. Integrating it into coding projects has transformed his understanding and confidence. Highly recommended!

course_guarantees.live_classes: Live, interactive classes with a real instructor, never pre-recorded videos.
course_guarantees.small_batches: Small batches only: group classes are capped at 10 students, with mini-batch (3 to 4 students) and personal 1-on-1 options.
course_guarantees.structured_curriculum: A structured, well-paced curriculum taught step by step, with hands-on practice in every session.
course_guarantees.doubt_support: Doubt support between classes over WhatsApp, so you are never left stuck.
course_guarantees.certificate: A course-completion certificate you can share.
course_guarantees.free_demo: A free demo class before you enrol, so you can decide with no pressure.
